skill.name: google-agents-cli-workflow
skill.description: Development workflow and guidelines for building, evaluating, deploying, and monitoring agents using Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) and the agents-cli toolkit.
skill.summary: Step-by-step ADK agent development workflow from Google's official agents-cli toolkit.
skill.author: google

security_audit.summary: This skill is pure markdown documentation from Google's official google/agents-cli repository. It contains no executable code — only workflow guidelines, CLI command references, and best practices for ADK agent development. All 229 static findings are false positives: backticks are markdown inline-code formatting (not shell execution), URLs point to official Google documentation, and .env/API-key references are educational discussions about configuration management.

low_findings.title: Extensive shell command examples in documentation
low_findings.description: The skill documents numerous CLI commands (agents-cli, uv, terraform, git) intended for the user to run manually. These are inline code-formatted examples in markdown, not executable code. No injection risk since the skill itself does not execute any commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct verification: the entire skill is markdown documentation with backtick-formatted command examples. No code execution path exists — the skill provides guidance for the AI assistant to relay to the user.

low_findings.title: References to .env files and environment variables
low_findings.description: The skill discusses .env file handling and environment variable best practices (GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT, GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION) as configuration guidance. This is educational documentation, not actual credential access by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content explicitly recommends GCP Secret Manager over plain .env entries for secrets, demonstrating security-conscious guidance rather than risky behavior.

low_findings.title: External URLs to official Google documentation
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to legitimate Google properties: adk.dev, cloud.google.com, github.com/google/adk-samples, docs.astral.sh. No suspicious or untrusted endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs verified to belong to Google's official documentation infrastructure and the Astral (uv) project. No data exfiltration targets.

content.user_title: Build Google ADK agents with guided workflows
content.value_statement: Building ADK agents involves many steps — scaffolding, evaluating, deploying, and monitoring. This skill provides a structured workflow so you can develop agents correctly using Google's agents-cli toolkit without missing critical steps.
actual_capabilities: Guide you through the 8-phase ADK agent development lifecycle from understanding requirements to production observation
Recommend when to load companion skills (scaffold, adk-code, eval, deploy, publish, observability) at each phase
Provide reference samples to clone and study before writing your own agent code
Enforce code preservation rules to prevent unintended modifications to existing agent configurations
Document evaluation methodology to validate agent behavior end-to-end (not just code correctness)
Supply troubleshooting guidance including systematic debugging steps and loop-breaking rules
limitations: Does not execute commands itself — provides guidance for you or your AI assistant to act on
Requires agents-cli installed via uv tool install for the documented commands to work
Does not cover non-Google agent frameworks (LangChain, AutoGen, etc.)
Evaluation guidance applies specifically to ADK eval tooling, not generic pytest-based testing

use_cases.title: Scaffolding a new ADK agent project
use_cases.description: Start a new Google ADK agent project with the correct project structure, CI/CD configuration, and evaluation boilerplate from the beginning.
use_cases.target_user: Developers new to Google's Agent Development Kit

use_cases.title: Deploying an evaluated agent to production
use_cases.description: Move an agent from local testing through evaluation thresholds to deployment on Agent Runtime, Cloud Run, or GKE with proper CI/CD setup.
use_cases.target_user: ML engineers and DevOps teams deploying AI agents

use_cases.title: Debugging and iterating on agent behavior
use_cases.description: Use systematic debugging steps, verbose JSON event inspection, and the eval-fix loop to identify and fix agent behavior issues.
use_cases.target_user: AI engineers debugging agent tool trajectories and response quality

prompt_templates.title: Start a new ADK agent project
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to build a new agent using Google's ADK. The agent should help users [describe purpose]. Help me scaffold the project using agents-cli and walk me through the requirements questions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner initiating their first ADK project

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate an existing agent
prompt_templates.prompt: I've built an agent at [path]. Run agents-cli eval to validate its behavior. Start with 1-2 sample cases and iterate until quality thresholds are met.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy an agent to Cloud Run
prompt_templates.prompt: My agent has passed evaluation. Deploy it to Cloud Run using agents-cli deploy. Set up CI/CD with GitHub Actions and use Cloud SQL for session storage.

prompt_templates.title: Debug a failing agent evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: My eval run shows [describe failure]. Follow the systematic debugging process: reproduce, localize, fix one thing, verify, and add a guard eval case.

output_examples.input: I want to build a customer support agent that can look up order status and create tickets.
output_examples.output: Before scaffolding, I need to clarify a few requirements: What external APIs does the order lookup connect to? Should the agent remember conversation context across sessions? Is this a prototype or production deployment? Once you answer these, I will write the spec to .agents-cli-spec.md and proceed to Phase 1 (Study Reference Samples).

output_examples.input: Run agents-cli eval on my agent.
output_examples.output: Starting evaluation workflow. First, activating the eval skill for dataset schema and metrics guidance. Then running agents-cli eval generate to produce traces, followed by agents-cli eval grade to score against your eval_config.yaml. Results will show per-case metrics and failure clusters.

best_practices: Always complete Phase 0 (Understand) and get explicit user approval on .agents-cli-spec.md before scaffolding or coding
Use agents-cli eval to validate agent behavior — never write pytest tests that assert on LLM output content
Never change the model configuration unless the user explicitly requests it — preserve all existing config values during modifications
anti_patterns: Skipping the scaffold phase to set up a project manually — you will miss eval boilerplate, CI/CD config, and project conventions
Writing pytest tests that check for keywords in LLM responses — these are flaky because LLM outputs are non-deterministic
Changing multiple variables simultaneously during debugging — you will not know which change fixed or broke the issue

faq.question: What is agents-cli?
faq.answer: agents-cli is Google's CLI and skills toolkit for building, evaluating, and deploying agents on Google Cloud using the Agent Development Kit (ADK). It works with any coding agent including Claude Code and Codex.

faq.question: Do I need Google Cloud credentials to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides development workflow guidance that works locally. Google Cloud credentials are only needed when you reach the deploy phase and choose a Google Cloud deployment target.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without agents-cli installed?
faq.answer: Yes, the workflow guidance applies generally. However, the documented CLI commands require agents-cli to be installed via uv tool install google-agents-cli.

faq.question: How is agents-cli eval different from pytest?
faq.answer: pytest tests code correctness — imports work, functions return expected types. agents-cli eval tests agent behavior — response quality, tool usage, persona consistency, and safety compliance.

faq.question: Should I deploy before evaluating?
faq.answer: No. Always complete evaluation locally first. The skill enforces evaluation thresholds as a prerequisite to deployment in Phase 4.

faq.question: What deployment targets are supported?
faq.answer: Three targets are supported: Agent Runtime (formerly Vertex AI Agent Engine), Cloud Run, and GKE. The skill provides a decision matrix in the deploy companion skill.
